Why You Might Need Sewer Replacement

One of the most frustrating responsibilities of homeownership is managing unpleasant tasks such as sewerage problems. Broken sewer pipes not only create an offensive smell but also require careful cleanup. Hiring a reputable plumbing professional who knows how to pinpoint the cause and locate the sewer line damage helps mitigate the issue. 

The most common causes that may require you to replace sewer lines are tree roots, corroded pipes, clogged lines and extreme temperatures. Tree roots naturally follow water sources, and as such, they can clog, weaken and break the pipe structure. Magnesium and calcium buildup is another culprit that creates cracks and leaks. If left untreated, the corrosion gradually wears away at the surface of sewer pipes. 

Sewer lines are not equipped to handle foreign debris, such as paper towels, feminine products and cooking grease. Anything flushed into the system beyond human waste and toilet paper can cause a blockage that adds stress to the pipes, leading to breakage. Also, the freezing Chicago temperatures can cause pipes to expand and burst. 

Your best line of defense is to discuss the common problem areas with a reliable plumbing expert who can advise how to avoid situations that might lead to sewer replacement in Chicago. You may also want to consider signing up for a routine annual maintenance plan that includes a system inspection.

Sewer Replacement Services in Chicago

The certified plumbers who are part of the Plumbers 911 network arrive at your property equipped to assess your sewer line and location. They examine the lines with a camera connected to a flexible cable, and from there, they determine the appropriate course of action. In some cases, they may avoid traditional sewer replacement by relining the existing pipe with a sleeve that prevents further pipe cracks. 

Another price-conscious alternative is employing a trenchless technique that involves hydraulics breaking apart the damaged pipe and replacing it with a new section. When a complete sewer replacement is necessary, the plumbing experts must dig a trench to replace the existing system. They conduct all excavation according to OSHA standards.

title: Backflow Testing & Installation
author: ryan@faceless.media
content:
Home

Backflow Testing & Installation | Chicago, IL

If your bungalow or flat needs Backflow Testing & Installation in Chicago, IL, contact Plumbers 911 to resolve your issues quickly.

Proper backflow prevention in Chicago is essential to protect the community’s drinking water. Many of the more than nine million people that live in Chicagoland take for granted the pure, safe tap water that flows from their faucets. This precious commodity must be protected with proper safeguards. If left unprotected, the results can be disastrous. Plumbing professionals in Chicago are responsible for making reliably clean drinking water a reality for local residents and businesses. 

One of the primary ways that licensed and certified plumbers help preserve the purity and freshness of the community’s drinking water is by installing, inspecting, and maintaining backflow prevention devices. Backflow occurs when the normal flow of water is reversed in the drinking water system. Backflow is potentially dangerous when non-potable water — water that is contaminated with toxic chemicals or hazardous compounds — flows back into the drinking water system. 

This cross-contamination jeopardizes the health and well-being of the people in every single home or business that experiences the backflow issue. For homeowners, a backflow preventer is required or recommended in situations involving non-potable water systems such as irrigation systems, hot tubs, swimming pools, ponds, private fire suppression or sprinkler systems, and boiler systems. In general, a backflow prevention device is required to be installed on any water system that is connected to the drinking water system and has the potential for cross-contamination.

Causes of Pressure Variations in a Water Line

Backpressure is experienced when the drinking water system is connected to a system with higher water pressure levels. The undrinkable or non-potable water can force its way into the drinking water system if a backflow preventer is absent or if an existing backflow preventer is non-functional. 

A booster pump, a boiler system, or water from a line that is elevated thirty feet above grade can force contaminated water into the water lines. The homeowner whose home meets any of these conditions should be aware of the potential for contamination. On the other hand, negative pressure in the drinking water supply system is called backsiphonage. 

This condition arises when water flows back out of the residential water line and into the main water system at the street. In these scenarios, all the water can be sucked out of a home or business. Common causes of backsiphonage include:

  • Lower pressure in the main water line, such as when a fire truck pumps water out of the line to fight a fire or flush the line
  • A line repair or break occurring at a point lower than a service point

The backflow prevention device prevents water from traveling back into the drinking water system once it has been pulled out of or drained out of the drinking water system.  

Signs That Backflow or Backsiphonage Has Occurred

One of the main ways to learn that a backflow incident may have occurred is from the local news station reporting a main water line break, a fire in the area, or line flushes in the area or neighborhood. 

Residents of Chicago can expect their tap water to flow without interruption or sputtering. The water should be clean and clear, as well as pleasant-tasting and odorless. Water in the drinking water supply line may have been contaminated due to the failure of the backflow preventer if any of the following conditions are present:

  • Drinking water has yellow, brown, or pink discoloration.
  • The water has an odor that is stagnant or metallic.
  • Water flow is slow, interrupted, or sputtering.
  • Rust particles and sediment are visible in the water.
  • The water has an unpleasant or odd taste.

Contact a certified and licensed plumber if any of these signs are present in the drinking water.  

The Importance of Inspecting and Testing Backflow Prevention Devices

Backflow prevention devices are crucial for the safety and security of the drinking water system and public health. These health and safety concerns highlight the need to have backflow preventers inspected and tested annually to ensure that they are in proper working condition. 

The failure of a backflow preventer could be lethal to those affected by cross-contamination. Typically, irrigation backflow prevention devices are located outside the home or business in a green, brown, or black plastic box near the water meter.

The following problems that affect pipes and fittings may necessitate gas line replacement or repair services:

  • Leaks: Poorly maintained appliances and faulty valves can cause gas leaks. A gas leak is characterized by a pungent sulfur-like smell similar to rotten eggs. Gas suppliers add this scent to help users detect leaks. It’s essential to have a plumbing professional perform gas leak testing to prevent serious safety concerns.
  • Improperly Sized Fittings: Issues with fittings can occur due to poorly selected or improperly installed components. If ignored, this could lead to gas leaks that could threaten your health or endanger your household.
  • Cracked Seals: Over time, seals may crack or dry out due to wear and tear. This can create serious problems because gas lines rely heavily on these components. If you notice any issues, have a professional inspect the seals as soon as possible.
  • Corroded Connectors and Joints: The protective layers corrode as joints and connectors’ age and wear down, which can contribute to severe gas line problems.
  • Blockages: Blockages in the piping could be caused by a buildup of sediment or debris which could affect your appliances. Blockages can also slow down the system’s functioning rather than completely block it.

It’s not advisable to attempt carrying out gas repairs on your own. Gas line installation is a job that requires a professional plumber. DIY repairs can endanger you, your home and others around you. A professional plumber will do the following: 

  • Initial Visit:
    • This involves visiting your home to diagnose the issue by performing a gas leak test. For installation, the visit involves finding an ideal place to fit the gas line. 
  • Installation or Repair:
    • A professional plumber will secure the necessary permits to ensure that the job complies with Chicago’s building codes. They’ll also observe all safety protocols while performing gas line testing.
  • Finishing:
    • After installation, any appliances will be connected, and you’ll be briefed on safety procedures when using the system.

Arlington Heights is known for its 50-acre Lake Arlington and the parklands that surround it. A large body of water is a beautiful sight to see outdoors, but it is a horrible sight to see down in your basement. To keep water outside where it belongs, most modern homes have sump pumps installed. However, traditional sump pumps that run off an electrical outlet won't work if a storm knocks out power to your home. The good news is you can lower this risk by installing a sump pump battery backup.

Plumbers911 can help by referring you to a local plumber who has expertise in the installation, maintenance, and repair of sump pump battery backups. Call us today at 1-833-PLUM-911 (833)-758-6911 to get your sump pump upgraded with a battery backup.

Why You Might Need Sump Pump Battery Backup Installation

One risk that is often overlooked is when severe storms can knock out the power in an area for an extended period of time. If there's no power, then a regular sump pump can't operate, and your basement can flood. Sure, you can invest in an emergency generator to power your critical appliances, but generators are expensive and can't do the job in all scenarios. For example, if the power goes out while you're away from the house, then nobody will be home to start the generator.

A simple, cost-effective, and dependable solution is to install a sump pump that has a self-contained battery backup. So if the power goes out, the sump pump simply switches to the battery for power and keeps running. When the power comes back on, the battery automatically recharges itself and stands by, ready for the next power outage. For extra peace of mind, you can have a two-pump system installed. The primary pump can be a traditional design that runs off an electrical outlet, and the second pump can operate off a battery backup. This two-pump design not only protects your home during a power outage, it can even save a basement if the primary pump fails due to a mechanical issue.

Plumbers 911 can refer you to a plumber who is experienced in sump pump battery backup installation. While sump pump battery backups can be simple to install, it's best to have an experienced professional do the job to ensure it works properly whether the power in your home is on or off. Our plumbers in the Arlington Heights area can also help you design a one- or two-pump system that meets your needs and gives you peace of mind. 

If you already have a sump pump with a battery backup unit installed, our plumbers can perform routine maintenance and testing so you feel confident that your pump is ready for the next severe storm. If an existing sump pump with battery backup is suffering a mechanical issue, our contractors offer sump pump backup replacement.

Why You Might Need Water Leak Detection & Repair

A water leak is something that should never be ignored by a property owner or tenant. Unchecked water leaks can lead to the accumulation of fluids within building structures, and this can have a range of consequences including property damage and health hazards. Since most water pipes aren’t usually exposed, you might not know there’s a leak until you experience serious issues. 

A licensed plumber is trained to look for inconsistencies in plumbing system and correct any problems before they get worse. For one, accumulated water can weaken walls and support structures by leading to rot, and it can also ruin personal belongings, furniture and other interior contents if left unchecked for too long. In the most severe cases, structural collapse can occur because parts of the building have been so severely weakened over time. 

Water leaks are also costly. If piping systems don’t shut off the way they’re supposed to, or pipe leak repair hasn’t been performed on compromised areas, then water may continue to flow and create higher-than-average utility bills. Add this to potentially expensive structural damage and cleanup costs, and the price for delaying repair of a leak can become substantial. 

Mold is another big problem that necessitates immediate water leak repair. Dark, wet surfaces can become ideal breeding grounds for mold and mildew. Both of these organisms can cause severe respiratory issues and impact the health of your family, employees or customers.

When you need water leak detection and repair in Arlington Heights, call Plumbers 911 at 1-833-PLUM-911 (833-758-6911).

From Frontier Days in the summer to the tree lighting ceremony at Harmony Park in the winter, Arlington Heights offers lots of special events and reasons to celebrate. What's not so fun is returning home to find you have a water leak. Plumbing issues can disrupt your life, but a Plumbers 911 plumber can make the interruption short-lived. The plumbers in our network can find and repair leaks quickly to cut down on water damage and get your plumbing operational again.

Give Plumbers 911 a call at 1-833-PLUM-911 (833-758-6911) when you suspect a leak and we'll find a plumber for you to get it fixed promptly.

Why You Might Need Water Leak Detection & Repair

Regular wear and tear can cause pipes to leak, where the pipes can corrode, connections can get loose or brackets holding the pipes fall out of place. Other times, damage to the plumbing lines, such as frozen pipes or something hitting the plumbing lines, can also cause leaks.

No matter what causes them, they all lead to water damage, which gets worse the longer the leaks go undetected. Water leaks can ruin flooring, drywall and other materials inside your home. They can also cause mold to grow over time. 

Dripping faucets, running toilets and leaky P-traps under sinks are often easy to spot. You'll likely hear and see the drips soon after the leaks start. Leaky pipes hidden in the walls or a slab leak under your floor isn't as easy to see. You might notice staining, puddles or dampness. Low water pressure and high water bills are also signs of a hidden leak.

Water Leak Detection & Repair Services in Arlington Heights

A licensed plumber knows where leaks commonly happen, but they can also inspect all your plumbing to ensure they catch every possible source of leaking. They typically start with a visual inspection of exposed pipes, and they look for signs of leaks, such as stains on the ceiling or damp drywall. Plumbers also use specialty tools to help detect hidden leaks you may not notice at first glance. 

Once they find it, they'll immediately repair the source of the leak. For a leaking toilet, that might mean replacing some internal parts. Leaky faucets might need to be replaced, or the plumber might be able to replace just a few parts. Pipe leaks often require sections of the pipe to be replaced. Your plumber may need to cut into your drywall to repair leaky pipes behind the wall. Once they do their fixes, they'll test them to make sure everything is working properly.
